title: ASCILINE 2.0
tagline: Real-time ASCII art video streaming engine for the browser.
description: ASCILINE 2.0 is a web engine that converts live video streams or uploaded media into real-time ASCII art rendered directly in the browser — no plugins, no server-side processing, pure client-side canvas magic.
story: Started as a joke: what if you could watch a movie but it looked like a Matrix screensaver? The first version was a Python script. ASCILINE 2.0 is a proper browser engine — real-time, low-latency, and surprisingly watchable.
forWho: Developers, digital artists, and retro-tech enthusiasts who want to do something weird and interesting with video on the web.

detailedSpecs.architecture: Client-side only. requestAnimationFrame loop reads video frame pixels from a hidden canvas, converts pixel brightness to ASCII characters, and renders to a monospace text canvas overlay.
keyPatterns: Debounced resize handler for responsive ASCII grid
WebRTC getUserMedia for live webcam input
